case_title: AITOR E LOYMEDY VS. VERNON LEON SANDERS et al

docket_entries.description: PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOYMENDY, AITOR E AS TO DEFENDANT SANDERS, VERNON LEON AN I NDIVIDUAL ENTERPRISES RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO A BUSINESS ENTITY FORM UNKNOWN DOES 1 TO 20, INCLUSIVE SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR SEP-18-2009 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JUN-16-2009 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         SEP-03-2009

NTC OF MTN FOR CHANGE OF VENUE, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ENTERPRISES RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO A BUSINESS ENTITY FORM UNKNOWN HEARING SET FOR JUL-13-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
FEE PAID ON EX PARTE APPLIOCATION OF TIME TO SERVE PLEADING FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOYMENDY, AITOR E

OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE; MEMO P/A; DECLARATION OF SARGIS G ATANOUS FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOYMENDY, AITOR E
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT ENTERPRISE RENT-A CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO
REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE FILED BY DEFENDANT ENTERPRISE RENT-A CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO
FURTHER DECLARATION OF AUDREY SMITH IN SUPPORT OF DEFT ENTERPRISE'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE (C.C.P. 397) FILED BY DEFENDANT ENTERPRISE RENT-A CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ENTERPRISES RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE TRANSFERRING THE MATTER BACK TO MADERA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, WITH PREJUDICE. MOVING PAPERS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION IN THE WRONG COURT BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT SANDERS RESIDED IN SAN FRANCISCO WHEN PLAINTIFF COMMENCED THE INSTANT ACTION. MOTION DENIED BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES. FIRST, AN ANSWER SHOULD HAVE BEEN ON FILE WHEN DEFENDANT MOVED ON THAT BASIS. SEE BURON EQUIPMENT COMPANY, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665. SECOND, COUNSEL'S DECLARATIONS ARE SUBSTANTIVELY INSUFFICIENT. SEE LIEPPMAN V. LIEBER (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 914, 919. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-13-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Jul-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ENTERPRISES RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion for Change of Venue transferring the matter back to Madera County Superior Court. Appearances: Sargis G. Atanous, Esq. of The Dunnion Law Firm (831) 373-8035, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Plaintiff and opposing party. There are no other appearances. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION DENIED, with prejudice. MOVING PAPERS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION IN THE WRONG COURT BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT SANDERS RESIDED IN SAN FRANCISCO WHEN PLAINTIFF COMMENCED THE INSTANT ACTION. MOTION DENIED BASED ON CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES. FIRST, AN ANSWER SHOULD HAVE BEEN ON FILE WHEN DEFENDANT MOVED ON THAT BASIS. SEE BURON EQUIPMENT COMPANY, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665. SECOND, COUNSEL'S DECLARATIONS ARE SUBSTANTIVELY INSUFFICIENT. SEE LIEPPMAN V. LIEBER (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 914, 919. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
